notes:
MITRE is a US nonprofit that operates federally funded research and development centers (FFRDCs). In cybersecurity, MITRE created and maintains foundational identifier systems and frameworks: CVE (vulnerability IDs), CWE (weakness taxonomy), ATT&CK (adversary techniques), CAPEC (attack patterns), and ATLAS (AI threat landscape). When you see a CVE ID, CWE number, or ATT&CK technique — that's MITRE's work.

CISA contracts MITRE to operate the CVE Program. NVD (NIST) enriches CVE records with CVSS, CPE, and CWE data. CNAs (CVE Numbering Authorities) can assign CVE IDs under MITRE's program.

data.description: The canonical vulnerability identifier system, operated by MITRE under contract with CISA.
data.notes:
CVE is the canonical identifier — other advisories cross-reference CVEs. NVD (NIST) enriches CVE records with CVSS scores, CPE entries, and CWE mappings, but NVD has processing backlogs. Quality of CVE descriptions varies by CNA — some provide detailed technical analysis, others minimal information. The cvelistV5 GitHub repo contains raw JSON records organized by year and bucket directories.

Multiple representations: The same CVE record is available through four access methods — the cve.org website (text/html), the cvelistV5 GitHub web view (text/html showing JSON), raw GitHub download (application/json), and the CVE Services API (application/json). Use `?content_type=application/json` to filter to machine-readable results only. The children below have identical patterns but different weights and content_type values: the website (weight 100) is the primary human-facing resolution, while raw GitHub (weight 45) and API (weight 50) are machine-readable access methods.

examples.note: Log4Shell — highest-profile CVE in recent history

children.description: CVE JSON record on GitHub (web view)
